flavorText: Assassins are masters of infiltration and lethal opening strikes.

features.description: You have advantage on attack rolls against creatures that have not yet acted in combat, and any hit against a surprised creature is a critical hit.
You can create false identities and establish convincing personas for infiltration.
After studying a creature for 3 hours, you can flawlessly mimic its speech, writing, and behavior.
When you hit a surprised creature, it must make a Constitution save or take double damage from your attack.

assassin-assassinate.summary: Open combat before enemies act: advantage against creatures that have not taken a turn, with devastating payoff versus surprised targets.
assassin-assassinate.description: Assassinate rewards first-turn sequencing and ambush planning. Prioritize initiative, surprise setups, and target selection so your opener meaningfully swings the encounter.

assassin-infiltration-expertise.summary: You become markedly better at maintaining cover identities and operating inside hostile territory.
assassin-infiltration-expertise.description: Infiltration Expertise reinforces that Assassin is not only about damage; it is also about mission setup, false roles, and controlled entry into dangerous spaces.

assassin-imposter.summary: Your disguise and imitation game reaches a level that supports long-form deception rather than quick bluffs alone.
assassin-imposter.description: Imposter is an identity feature that should matter heavily in intrigue or covert campaigns where being believable is as valuable as dealing damage.

assassin-death-strike.summary: Convert a successful surprise hit into a potential double-damage execution window.
assassin-death-strike.description: Death Strike is a timing feature, not a passive buff. It pays off when your team can actually create surprise and you capitalize on the burst window immediately.
